title: Listing containers with docker compat API can leaks `stopped` state
body:
### Issue Description

The list container `/containers/json` endpoint (docker compat) can return `stopped` as the container state. This is a transient state, but this breaks the interface, which can cause libraries that depend on the docker API to break. I caught this while using bollard with a container that is getting restarted

```
Error: ReadState(Oci(Error { context: Some("failed to list containers"), source: Connection(JsonDataError { message: "unknown variant `stopped`, expected one of ``, `created`, `running`, `paused`, `restarting`, `exited`, `removing`, `dead` at line 1 column 707", column: 707 }) }))
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I don't have a reproduction since this state is temporary, however there is a clear bug in the tests, that are accepting `stopped` as a valid state.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/apiv2/20-containers.at#L70

The issue seems to be here in the `LibpodToContainer` function used by `ListContainers`

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/07aa62f82f4429d58304a8c72cecb38bf874e90e/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers.go#L310

while `GetContainer` returns the result of `LibpodToContainerJSON` that correctly re-maps the stopped state to exited here 

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/07aa62f82f4429d58304a8c72cecb38bf874e90e/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers.go#L486

there is no such conversion for `ListContainers` allowing the state to pass straight through

### Describe the results you received

`/containers/json` leaks `state: "stopped"`

### Describe the results you expected

states should match what's returned by `/containers/{name}/json` and not leak internal implementation dependent states

### podman info output

### Upstream Latest Release

Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: An additional point is that while originally the docker API did not specify an interface for the `state` field, that has been corrected on 1.48 and forward https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version/v1.48/#tag/Container/operation/ContainerList
Found this related issue #17728, that was fixed but only for container inspect, not for list.
